steps.title: Identify the structures after the blank
steps.content: After the blank, two independent clauses appear separated by a comma: “the hole's small size restricts light to a single ray” (subject: the hole's small size; verb: restricts) and “all light passing through the hole can only arrive at a single destination” (subject: all light; verb: can arrive).

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: Two independent clauses cannot be joined by a comma alone—they require a period, semicolon, or coordinating conjunction. Choice B solves this by using a period to create two sentences; the second begins with a dependent clause “Because the hole's small size restricts light to a single ray” that properly introduces the following independent clause. Choices A, C, and D each create comma splices or fail to properly separate the independent clauses. That matches choice B — B is correct.
